rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference

Leave-k-out jackknife stability analysis for trait-exclusive cliques

Source: R/cliques.R
clique_stability.Rd

Tests how robust each clique's trait exclusivity is to species removal. A clique is trait-exclusive if all its species share the same discrete trait value (e.g., all "annual" or all "perennial"). The analysis removes k = 1, 2, ..., max_k species at a time, re-runs full clique detection on each reduced species set, and checks whether exclusivity is preserved.

clique_stability(
  edges,
  target_species,
  species_trait,
  all_species = target_species,
  full_cliques = NULL,
  min_species = length(target_species),
  max_k = length(all_species) - 2L,
  max_genes_per_sp = 10L,
  jaccard_threshold = 0.8,
  edge_type = "conserved",
  n_cores = 1L
)

Arguments

edges

Data frame (same format as find_cliques).

target_species

Character vector of species that define clique membership (e.g., the 4 annuals).

species_trait

Named character or factor vector mapping species to trait groups. Names must include all all_species. Example: c(SP_A = "annual", SP_B = "perennial").

all_species

Character vector of ALL species in the analysis universe (default: target_species). Leave-k-out subsets are drawn from all_species. Must be a superset of target_species. When larger than target_species, removing a non-target species tests whether the clique signal is robust to changes in the broader phylogenetic context.

full_cliques

Output of find_cliques, or NULL to compute internally (default).

min_species

Minimum species per clique in the full dataset (default: length(target_species)). During leave-k-out, reduced cliques require only 2 species (the minimum meaningful clique size); a clique is testable if at least 2 of its species remain active.

max_k

Maximum number of species to leave out (default: length(all_species) - 2, leaving at least 2 species).

max_genes_per_sp

Maximum genes per species per HOG (default 10).

jaccard_threshold

Minimum Jaccard similarity for matching reduced-dataset cliques to full-dataset cliques (default 0.8).

edge_type

Edge type filter (default "conserved").

n_cores

Number of OpenMP threads (default 1).

Value

A list with components:

stability

Data frame with columns: clique_idx, hog, trait_value, k, n_subsets, n_stable, stability_score, sole_rep

clique_disruption

Data frame with columns: species, trait_value, n_cliques_disrupted (k=1 only). One row per species in all_species.

stability_class

Named integer vector: highest k at which each exclusive clique is stable across ALL subsets (0 = unstable at k=1)

novel_cliques

Integer: total count of novel cliques across all subsets

Details

The trait system is generic: any discrete species-level trait with any number of levels. For example, life habit (annual/perennial), climate zone (tropical/temperate/arctic), or ploidy level (2n/4n/6n).

How it works

For each combination of k species removed from all_species (k = 1, ..., max_k):

  1. All edges involving the removed species are dropped

  2. Clique detection re-runs among remaining target species

  3. Each resulting clique is annotated by trait exclusivity

  4. Reduced cliques are matched to full-dataset cliques by Jaccard similarity of gene assignments (considering only non-removed species)

  5. For matched cliques, trait exclusivity preservation is checked

A clique is testable in a subset if at least 2 of its species remain active. It is stable at level k if its trait exclusivity is preserved across ALL C(N, k) subsets where it is testable.

When all_species is larger than target_species, subsets may remove non-target species. With static edges, removing a non-target species does not change target-species edges, so these subsets are trivially stable. This becomes meaningful when edge q-values are re-computed per subset (future extension).

sole_rep column

The sole_rep column in the stability data frame is TRUE if this clique's trait value has only one target species representative, meaning removal of that species trivially breaks exclusivity.

full_cliques parameter

When full_cliques = NULL (default), cliques are computed internally via find_cliques. You can also precompute them:


fc <- find_cliques(edges, target_species)
stab <- clique_stability(edges, target_species, species_trait,
                         full_cliques = fc)

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Find annual-exclusive cliques stable across all 8 Poaceae species
annual_sp    <- c("BDIS", "HVUL", "BMAX", "VBRO")
perennial_sp <- c("BSYL", "HJUB", "BMED", "FPRA")
all_sp       <- c(annual_sp, perennial_sp)
trait <- setNames(rep(c("annual", "perennial"), each = 4), all_sp)

cliques <- find_cliques(edges, annual_sp)
stab    <- clique_stability(edges, annual_sp, trait,
                            all_species = all_sp,
                            full_cliques = cliques)
# max_k defaults to 6 (= 8 - 2), testing k = 1..6

# Cliques surviving any single species dropout
k1 <- stab$stability[stab$stability$k == 1, ]
stable_cliques <- cliques[k1$clique_idx[k1$stability_score == 1] + 1L, ]

# Multi-level: stability_class >= 2 survives any pair of dropouts
deeply_stable <- which(stab$stability_class >= 2)
} # }
